title: genproc: deep dive
text: synthetic file-conversion task README — one row per file, convert() per-case function.
code: src_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "genproc-vignette-src") dst_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "genproc-vignette-dst") dir.create(src_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE) dir.create(dst_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)  write.csv(head(iris),       file.path(src_dir, "a.csv"), row.names = FALSE) write.csv(head(mtcars),     file.path(src_dir, "b.csv"), row.names = FALSE) write.csv(head(airquality), file.path(src_dir, "c.csv"), row.names = FALSE)  convert <- function(src_dir, src_file, dst_dir, dst_file) {   df <- read.csv(file.path(src_dir, src_file))   saveRDS(df, file.path(dst_dir, dst_file)) }  mask <- data.frame(   src_dir  = src_dir,   src_file = c("a.csv", "b.csv", "c.csv"),   dst_dir  = dst_dir,   dst_file = c("a.rds", "b.rds", "c.rds"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  result <- genproc(convert, mask)

title: genproc: deep dive
text: result S3 list stable, documented set fields: log: one row per case. Columns, order: case_id, mask parameter values (order appear mask), success, error_message, traceback, duration_secs. reproducibility: environment snapshot captured run start (see ). n_success, n_error: summary counts. duration_total_secs: total wall-clock time. status: "done" synchronous run, "running" "error" non-blocking run await(). fields guaranteed stable across minor versions; new fields may added (e.g. worker_id parallel runs), existing ones never removed renamed.
code: class(result) #> [1] "genproc_result" names(result) #> [1] "log"                 "reproducibility"     "n_success"           #> [4] "n_error"             "duration_total_secs" "status"

title: genproc: deep dive
text: Column order designed human scanning run: case_id stable index-based (case_0001, case_0002, …) now. content-based variant roadmap, use cases rows mask can reordered runs.
code: result$log #>     case_id                              src_dir src_file #> 1 case_0001 /tmp/Rtmpj7mT7Z/genproc-vignette-src    a.csv #> 2 case_0002 /tmp/Rtmpj7mT7Z/genproc-vignette-src    b.csv #> 3 case_0003 /tmp/Rtmpj7mT7Z/genproc-vignette-src    c.csv #>                                dst_dir dst_file success error_message traceback #> 1 /tmp/Rtmpj7mT7Z/genproc-vignette-dst    a.rds    TRUE          <NA>      <NA> #> 2 /tmp/Rtmpj7mT7Z/genproc-vignette-dst    b.rds    TRUE          <NA>      <NA> #> 3 /tmp/Rtmpj7mT7Z/genproc-vignette-dst    c.rds    TRUE          <NA>      <NA> #>   duration_secs #> 1         0.001 #> 2         0.001 #> 3         0.001

title: genproc: deep dive
text: timestamp: POSIXct, start time run. r_version, platform, os, locale, timezone: system info. packages: named character vector package -> version, every package attached loaded via namespace run start. mask_snapshot: exact mask used (copy reference — value). parallel: NULL sequential run, plain list mirroring parallel_spec() used. Dropped class make snapshot portable serialization formats. nonblocking: pattern, nonblocking_spec(). inputs: fingerprint every file mask refers , NULL input tracking disabled (track_inputs = FALSE). See next section. snapshot lives inside result. can compare two results comparing $reproducibility slots directly.
code: str(result$reproducibility, max.level = 1) #> List of 11 #>  $ timestamp    : POSIXct[1:1], format: "2026-04-28 19:14:39" #>  $ r_version    : chr "R version 4.6.0 (2026-04-24)" #>  $ platform     : chr "x86_64-pc-linux-gnu" #>  $ os           : chr "Linux 6.17.0-1011-azure" #>  $ locale       : chr "LC_CTYPE=C.UTF-8;LC_NUMERIC=C;LC_TIME=C.UTF-8;LC_COLLATE=C.UTF-8;LC_MONETARY=C.UTF-8;LC_MESSAGES=C;LC_PAPER=C.U"| __truncated__ #>  $ timezone     : chr "UTC" #>  $ packages     : Named chr [1:33] "0.0.0.9000" "0.6.39" "1.4.3" "2.6.1" ... #>   ..- attr(*, "names")= chr [1:33] "genproc" "digest" "desc" "R6" ... #>  $ mask_snapshot:'data.frame':   3 obs. of  4 variables: #>  $ parallel     : NULL #>  $ nonblocking  : NULL #>  $ inputs       :List of 3

title: genproc: deep dive
text: reproducibility$inputs layer protects silent drift upstream files. captured t0 run, alongside rest snapshot. method: currently "stat". Reserved future extensions (e.g. "md5" content hashing). files: deduplicated table every file referenced mask, size bytes last-modified time. One row per unique path: config file shared across 100 cases produces single row. refs: (case_id, column, path) triples saying referenced . Joins back files path.
code: str(result$reproducibility$inputs, max.level = 1) #> List of 3 #>  $ method: chr "stat" #>  $ files :'data.frame':  0 obs. of  3 variables: #>  $ refs  :'data.frame':  0 obs. of  3 variables:

title: genproc: deep dive
text: default, every character column mask whose non-NA values existing files (contain path separator) treated input column. mask used vignette paths split across src_dir src_file, heuristic finds nothing useful — src_dir directory (excluded), src_file values bare names (separator). mask holds absolute paths directly:
code: mask_paths <- data.frame(   csv_in = file.path(src_dir, c("a.csv", "b.csv", "c.csv")),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) do_one <- function(csv_in) nrow(read.csv(csv_in))  run0 <- genproc(do_one, mask_paths) run0$reproducibility$inputs$files #>                                         path size               mtime #> 1 /tmp/Rtmpj7mT7Z/genproc-vignette-src/a.csv  214 2026-04-28 19:14:39 #> 2 /tmp/Rtmpj7mT7Z/genproc-vignette-src/b.csv  296 2026-04-28 19:14:39 #> 3 /tmp/Rtmpj7mT7Z/genproc-vignette-src/c.csv  154 2026-04-28 19:14:39

title: genproc: deep dive
text: genproc(..., input_cols = c("col1", "col2")) bypasses heuristic tracks exactly named columns. Paths don’t exist capture time recorded NA size/mtime warning emitted. genproc(..., skip_input_cols = c("col")) keeps heuristic excludes column (useful label column happens match existing file cwd). genproc(..., track_inputs = FALSE) disables tracking entirely. result$reproducibility$inputs NULL. input_cols skip_input_cols mutually exclusive. Mixing raises error — two flags express contradictory intentions call clarify.

title: genproc: deep dive
text: diff_inputs() returns S3 object (genproc_input_diff) print method human reading named list components programmatic access ($changed, $unchanged, $removed, $added). Files matched canonical absolute path; cross-machine comparison need separate matcher scope version. diff_inputs() refuses compare snapshots produced different methods (forward-compatible future hash mode).
code: # Rewrite a.csv with strictly more content (size changes) write.csv(iris, file.path(src_dir, "a.csv"), row.names = FALSE)  run1 <- genproc(do_one, mask_paths) diff_inputs(run0, run1) #> genproc input diff (method: stat) #>   Changed:   1 #>   Unchanged: 2 #>   Removed:   0 #>   Added:     0 #>  #> Changed files: #>   /tmp/Rtmpj7mT7Z/genproc-vignette-src/a.csv #>       size:  214 B -> 3.9 KB #>       mtime: 2026-04-28 19:14:39 -> 2026-04-28 19:14:39

title: genproc: deep dive
text: case throws stop run. delete source file two runs: failing row carries error message filtered traceback: traceback captured via withCallingHandlers() moment error thrown — real R call stack, string pulled conditionMessage(). internal tryCatch() withCallingHandlers() frames filtered trace reads like normal R error. Restore file subsequent sections:
code: file.remove(file.path(src_dir, "b.csv")) #> [1] TRUE result_broken <- genproc(convert, mask) #> Warning in file(file, "rt"): cannot open file #> '/tmp/Rtmpj7mT7Z/genproc-vignette-src/b.csv': No such file or directory  result_broken$n_success #> [1] 2 result_broken$n_error #> [1] 1 bad <- result_broken$log[!result_broken$log$success, ] bad$error_message #> [1] "cannot open the connection" cat(bad$traceback[1], "\n") #> 1. read.csv(file.path(src_dir, src_file)) #> 2. read.table(file = file, header = header, sep = sep, quote = quote, dec = dec, fill = fill, comment.char = comment.ch ... #> 3. file(file, "rt") write.csv(head(mtcars), file.path(src_dir, "b.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

title: genproc: deep dive
text: function already uses parameter names don’t match mask’s column names, f_mapping renames place without touching source:
code: # `f` uses generic names; the mask uses domain names. f <- function(input_dir, input_file, output_dir, output_file) {   df <- read.csv(file.path(input_dir, input_file))   saveRDS(df, file.path(output_dir, output_file)) }  genproc(   f = f,   mask = mask,   f_mapping = c(     "input_dir"   = "src_dir",     "input_file"  = "src_file",     "output_dir"  = "dst_dir",     "output_file" = "dst_file"   ) )

title: genproc: deep dive
text: parallel_spec() records intent ; actual workers started lazily future plan resolved.

what: Power-user pattern: manage the plan yourself
title: genproc: deep dive
text: Across many genproc() calls session, installing plan amortizes worker startup:
code: future::plan(future::multisession, workers = 6)  result_1 <- genproc(convert, mask, parallel = parallel_spec()) result_2 <- genproc(convert, mask, parallel = parallel_spec()) # reuses the same workers

what: One-off pattern: let genproc install the plan
title: genproc: deep dive
text: workers passed without strategy, genproc() auto-defaults multisession single call restores previous plan exit. avoids silent trap workers = N ignored current plan sequential:
code: genproc(convert, mask, parallel = parallel_spec(workers = 4))

title: genproc: deep dive
text: multisession costs roughly 1–3 seconds per worker first use. ~10 seconds real work, parallel run can slower sequential one. Don’t read single short benchmark evidence bug: run workload size.

title: genproc: deep dive
text: parallel_spec(seed = TRUE) (default) draws independent L’Ecuyer-CMRG streams random master seed. Identical master seed — identical per-case RNG state regardless worker count chunking. pin master, pass integer: parallel_spec(seed = 42L).

title: genproc: deep dive
code: job <- genproc(convert, mask, nonblocking = nonblocking_spec()) status(job)         # "running" or "done" job <- await(job)   # blocks until resolution job$log

title: genproc: deep dive
text: await(): log, n_success, n_error, duration_total_secs NULL. reproducibility already populated — snapshot captured synchronously, future submitted, reflects t0. status "running". future stored attr(x, "future"). await(): fields populated synchronous run. attr(x, "future") removed. status "done" — "error" wrapper future crashed (rare case: per-case errors caught logging layer don’t propagate wrapper).

title: genproc: deep dive
text: await() idempotent. Calling object already materialized (synchronous begin ) returns unchanged. makes safe pepper user code without tracking whether particular result already collected.

title: genproc: deep dive
text: Unlike parallel_spec(), nonblocking_spec() defaults strategy = "multisession", NULL. Rationale: function named “non-blocking” must silently block current future::plan() sequential (default fresh R session). Power-users manage plan can pass strategy = NULL explicitly defer.

title: genproc: deep dive
text: nonblocking_spec() installs plan, previous plan restored genproc() exit — restored await() future collected. Restoring earlier call future::plan() wrapper future still running, shuts multisession workers surfaces “Future canceled” error collection time. trade-never call await(), installed plan stays active rest session. Power-users pass strategy = NULL manage plan affected.

title: genproc: deep dive
text: two optional layers orthogonal: non-blocking layer launches one outer future. Inside , parallel layer dispatches cases via future.apply. Note: set multisession, future.apply detects already inside future degrades inner layer sequential default, avoid worker explosion. true nested parallelism, install future::plan(list(...)) explicitly pass strategy = NULL specs.
code: job <- genproc(   convert, mask,   parallel    = parallel_spec(workers = 6),   nonblocking = nonblocking_spec() ) # get control back immediately # ... do other work ... job <- await(job)

title: genproc: deep dive
text: yet package, explicitly planned: Content-hash input fingerprinting: current inputs layer uses stat-based fingerprint (size + mtime), detects every legitimate file modification can fooled adversary preserves . method = "md5" ("xxhash64") opt-reserved API land later. Content-based case_id: today case IDs index-based. content-based variant make replay stable even mask rows reordered. Error replay: replay(result, case_id) rerun one failed case isolation. Monitored progress: opt-progress reporting survives non-blocking parallel modes. cancel() non-blocking: backend-dependent, deferred. architecture designed adding layers require changes existing user code — new layers composed extra arguments genproc(), extra fields genproc_result accumulate without removing existing ones.

what: Robust, Logged and Reproducible Iteration at Organizational Scale
title: Robust, Logged and Reproducible Iteration at Organizational Scale
text: genproc turns one-iterative R procedures (, lapply(), purrr::pmap(), …) production-grade workflows wrapping orthogonal, composable execution layers. goal make transition “script works one case” “system runs reliably many cases” architectural step, improvised rewrite. Two layers always active disabled: Logged — case produces structured log row real traceback (captured via withCallingHandlers()) per-case timing. Reproducibility — run records R version, loaded package versions, execution environment, exact iteration mask, spec optional layer used, two runs can compared parameter drift auditable. Two optional layers can composed defaults, caller’s choice: Parallel execution via future ecosystem (future.apply::future_lapply()). Non-blocking execution: genproc() returns immediately genproc_result status "running" run continues background future. Poll status(), block await(). genproc zero Shiny dependency. companion package (genprocShiny) later build UI top functions.

title: Robust, Logged and Reproducible Iteration at Organizational Scale
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("danielrak/genproc")

title: Robust, Logged and Reproducible Iteration at Organizational Scale
text: toy file-conversion task: read CSVs one directory, save RDS another. One case per file. Every run returns genproc_result. log contains one row per case, stable case_id, parameter values, success, error_message, traceback, duration_secs: case fails, run continues — error captured, thrown. delete one source file purpose second run: traceback column holds real R call stack failing case (filtered tryCatch/withCallingHandlers machinery), debugging failed case reads like normal R error.
code: library(genproc)  # Synthetic workspace src_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "src") dst_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "dst") dir.create(src_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE) dir.create(dst_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)  write.csv(head(iris),       file.path(src_dir, "a.csv"), row.names = FALSE) write.csv(head(mtcars),     file.path(src_dir, "b.csv"), row.names = FALSE) write.csv(head(airquality), file.path(src_dir, "c.csv"), row.names = FALSE)  # Per-case function convert <- function(src_dir, src_file, dst_dir, dst_file) {   df <- read.csv(file.path(src_dir, src_file))   saveRDS(df, file.path(dst_dir, dst_file)) }  # Iteration mask: one row per conversion mask <- data.frame(   src_dir  = src_dir,   src_file = c("a.csv", "b.csv", "c.csv"),   dst_dir  = dst_dir,   dst_file = c("a.rds", "b.rds", "c.rds"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  result <- genproc(convert, mask) result$log[, c("case_id", "src_file", "dst_file",                "success", "duration_secs")] #>     case_id src_file dst_file success duration_secs #> 1 case_0001    a.csv    a.rds    TRUE             0 #> 2 case_0002    b.csv    b.rds    TRUE             0 #> 3 case_0003    c.csv    c.rds    TRUE             0 file.remove(file.path(src_dir, "b.csv")) #> [1] TRUE  result2 <- genproc(convert, mask) #> Warning in file(file, "rt"): impossible d'ouvrir le fichier #> 'C:\Users\rheri\AppData\Local\Temp\RtmpegrrE4/src/b.csv' : No such file or #> directory result2$log[result2$log$success == FALSE,             c("case_id", "src_file", "error_message")] #>     case_id src_file                    error_message #> 2 case_0002    b.csv impossible d'ouvrir la connexion result2$n_success #> [1] 2 result2$n_error #> [1] 1

title: Robust, Logged and Reproducible Iteration at Organizational Scale
text: result$reproducibility plain list recording R version, OS, timezone, loaded package versions, exact mask used, specs optional layer, fingerprint every input file referenced mask. snapshot lives inside result — side file keep sync:
code: str(result$reproducibility, max.level = 1) #> List of 11 #>  $ timestamp    : POSIXct[1:1], format: "2026-04-28 19:37:59" #>  $ r_version    : chr "R version 4.5.1 (2025-06-13 ucrt)" #>  $ platform     : chr "x86_64-w64-mingw32" #>  $ os           : chr "Windows 10 x64" #>  $ locale       : chr "LC_COLLATE=French_France.utf8;LC_CTYPE=French_France.utf8;LC_MONETARY=French_France.utf8;LC_NUMERIC=C;LC_TIME=F"| __truncated__ #>  $ timezone     : chr "Europe/Paris" #>  $ packages     : Named chr [1:22] "0.0.0.9000" "4.5.1" "1.2.0" "3.6.5" ... #>   ..- attr(*, "names")= chr [1:22] "genproc" "compiler" "fastmap" "cli" ... #>  $ mask_snapshot:'data.frame':   3 obs. of  4 variables: #>  $ parallel     : NULL #>  $ nonblocking  : NULL #>  $ inputs       :List of 3

title: Robust, Logged and Reproducible Iteration at Organizational Scale
text: result$reproducibility$inputs records size mtime every input file mask refers . intent flag common reproducibility failure: re-running code paths upstream file silently rewritten. genproc detects input columns automatically: character column mask whose values existing files treated . Pass track_inputs = FALSE skip capture, override heuristic input_cols = c(...) (force) skip_input_cols = c(...) (exclude). diff_inputs() compares two runs tells referenced files changed since first one: default method "stat" (size + mtime). detects every legitimate modification near-zero cost; content-hash variant roadmap stronger guarantees adversarial workloads.
code: mask_paths <- data.frame(   csv_in = file.path(src_dir, c("a.csv", "b.csv", "c.csv")),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) do_one <- function(csv_in) nrow(read.csv(csv_in))  run0 <- genproc(do_one, mask_paths) #> Warning in file(file, "rt"): impossible d'ouvrir le fichier #> 'C:\Users\rheri\AppData\Local\Temp\RtmpegrrE4/src/b.csv' : No such file or #> directory run0$reproducibility$inputs$files #> [1] path  size  mtime #> <0 lignes> (ou 'row.names' de longueur nulle) # Rewrite a.csv with strictly more content (size changes) write.csv(iris, file.path(src_dir, "a.csv"), row.names = FALSE)  run1 <- genproc(do_one, mask_paths) #> Warning in file(file, "rt"): impossible d'ouvrir le fichier #> 'C:\Users\rheri\AppData\Local\Temp\RtmpegrrE4/src/b.csv' : No such file or #> directory diff_inputs(run0, run1) #> genproc input diff (method: stat) #>   Changed:   0 #>   Unchanged: 0 #>   Removed:   0 #>   Added:     0

title: Robust, Logged and Reproducible Iteration at Organizational Scale
text: Dispatch cases across workers passing parallel_spec(): two mandatory layers remain active parallel mode. Case order preserved log regardless order workers return. hood, parallel_spec() builds configuration object consumed future.apply::future_lapply(). manage future::plan() (recommended across several calls amortize worker startup), pass parallel_spec() without strategy current plan used unchanged.
code: # Four workers, a temporary multisession plan, restored on exit result <- genproc(   convert, mask,   parallel = parallel_spec(workers = 4) )

title: Robust, Logged and Reproducible Iteration at Organizational Scale
text: Return immediately, keep console, collect later: nonblocking_spec() can composed parallel_spec() — non-blocking wrapper envelops parallel dispatch:
code: job <- genproc(   convert, mask,   nonblocking = nonblocking_spec() )  status(job)        # "running" or "done" job <- await(job)  # blocks until resolution job$log job <- genproc(   convert, mask,   parallel    = parallel_spec(workers = 4),   nonblocking = nonblocking_spec() )

what: Wrap a function with error handling and a structured log row — add_trycatch_logrow
title: Wrap a function with error handling and a structured log row — add_trycatch_logrow
text: Takes function returns modified version : always returns one-row data.frame ("log row") captures errors without stopping, recording error message real traceback (call stack point failure) records wall-clock execution time

title: Wrap a function with error handling and a structured log row — add_trycatch_logrow
code: add_trycatch_logrow(f)

title: Wrap a function with error handling and a structured log row — add_trycatch_logrow
text: f function wrap.

title: Wrap a function with error handling and a structured log row — add_trycatch_logrow
text: function formals f, whose return value one-row data.frame columns: original arguments, success (logical), error_message (character NA), traceback (character NA), duration_secs (numeric).

title: Wrap a function with error handling and a structured log row — add_trycatch_logrow
text: tryCatch() unwinds call stack entering error handler. means sys.calls() inside tryCatch error handler returns handler's stack, stack led error. function uses withCallingHandlers() (fires original stack still intact) capture traceback, lets error propagate outer tryCatch() control flow.

title: Wrap a function with error handling and a structured log row — add_trycatch_logrow
text: returned data.frame always one row. Columns: One column per formal argument f, value passed success: TRUE body executed without error error_message: error's conditionMessage(), NA traceback: formatted call stack error, NA duration_secs: wall-clock seconds elapsed

title: Wrap a function with error handling and a structured log row — add_trycatch_logrow
code:
safe_sqrt <- add_trycatch_logrow(function(x) sqrt(x)) safe_sqrt(4)    # success = TRUE, duration_secs > 0 #>   x success error_message traceback duration_secs #> 1 4    TRUE          <NA>      <NA>             0 safe_sqrt("a")  # success = FALSE, error_message filled, traceback filled #>   x success                                 error_message #> 1 a   FALSE non-numeric argument to mathematical function #>                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       traceback #> 1 1. build_site(pkg, preview = FALSE, install = install, new_process = new_process, ...)
2. build_site_local(pkg = pkg, examples = examples, run_dont_run = run_dont_run, seed = seed, lazy = lazy, override = o ...
3. build_reference(pkg, lazy = lazy, examples = examples, run_dont_run = run_dont_run, seed = seed, override = override ...
4. unwrap_purrr_error(purrr::map(topics, build_reference_topic, pkg = pkg, lazy = lazy, examples_env = examples_env, ru ... #>   duration_secs #> 1             0

what: Block until a non-blocking genproc run has resolved — await
title: Block until a non-blocking genproc run has resolved — await
text: await() blocks background future non-blocking genproc() run resolved, returns genproc_result log, n_success, n_error, duration_total_secs, status populated. wrapper future crashed (rare case — user errors inside individual cases caught logging layer wrapper crashes), returned object status = "error" populated error_message.

title: Block until a non-blocking genproc run has resolved — await
code: await(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'genproc_result' await(x, ...)

title: Block until a non-blocking genproc run has resolved — await
text: x object. Methods exist genproc_result. ... Unused, future extensions.

title: Block until a non-blocking genproc run has resolved — await
text: genproc_result status != "running".

title: Block until a non-blocking genproc run has resolved — await
text: await() idempotent: calling result already materialized (synchronous begin ) returns unchanged.

what: Compare input file fingerprints between two genproc runs — diff_inputs
title: Compare input file fingerprints between two genproc runs — diff_inputs
text: Takes two genproc_result objects produced genproc() (function mask, run two different times) reports referenced input files changed since first run.

title: Compare input file fingerprints between two genproc runs — diff_inputs
code: diff_inputs(r0, r1)

title: Compare input file fingerprints between two genproc runs — diff_inputs
text: r0, r1 Two genproc_result objects. convention, r0 earlier run r1 later one, function symmetric respect changed / unchanged. labels removed (present r0, absent r1) added (opposite) follow asymmetric convention.

title: Compare input file fingerprints between two genproc runs — diff_inputs
text: object class genproc_input_diff (named list) components: method Character, e.g. "stat". changed data.frame columns path, size_before, size_after, mtime_before, mtime_after. One row per file whose size mtime differs. unchanged Character vector paths whose size mtime identical runs. removed Character vector paths present r0's snapshot absent r1's. added Character vector paths present r1's snapshot absent r0's.

title: Compare input file fingerprints between two genproc runs — diff_inputs
text: Files matched canonical absolute path. method field must agree two runs.

title: Compare input file fingerprints between two genproc runs — diff_inputs
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   r0 <- genproc(my_fn, my_mask)   # ... edit one of the input CSVs ...   r1 <- genproc(my_fn, my_mask)   diff_inputs(r0, r1) } # }

what: Transform an example expression into a parameterized function — from_example_to_function
title: Transform an example expression into a parameterized function — from_example_to_function
text: Takes concrete R expression (e.g. data processing script works one specific case) returns function every external value (strings, variables environment functions) replaced named parameter original value default.

title: Transform an example expression into a parameterized function — from_example_to_function
code: from_example_to_function(expr, env = parent.frame())

title: Transform an example expression into a parameterized function — from_example_to_function
text: expr expression length 1, typically created expression() quote() wrapped .expression(). env environment look symbols. Symbols found environment functions turned parameters. Defaults caller's environment.

title: Transform an example expression into a parameterized function — from_example_to_function
text: function whose body rewritten expression whose formals detected parameters default values. function's environment set env.

title: Transform an example expression into a parameterized function — from_example_to_function
text: first step genproc workflow: user writes working example, from_example_to_function() extracts reusable, parameterized version .

title: Transform an example expression into a parameterized function — from_example_to_function
text: String literals: every string expression becomes parameter (e.g. "output.csv" -> param_1 default "output.csv"). Non-function symbols: symbol exists env value function, becomes parameter.

title: Transform an example expression into a parameterized function — from_example_to_function
text: Locally bound symbols: variables created assignments inside expression (e.g. result <- ...) never parameterized. Function names: head call (e.g. read.csv read.csv(path)) never parameterized. Functions environment: symbols whose value function assumed part program structure, data. Numeric, logical, NULL, non-character atomic values.

title: Transform an example expression into a parameterized function — from_example_to_function
text: value produces parameter. "output.csv" appears twice, occurrences map param_N.

title: Transform an example expression into a parameterized function — from_example_to_function
code: # --- Basic usage --- input_path <- "/data/input.csv"  expr <- expression({   df <- read.csv(input_path)   write.csv(df, "output.csv") })  fn <- from_example_to_function(expr) fn #> function (param_1 = "/data/input.csv", param_2 = "output.csv")  #> { #>     df <- read.csv(param_1) #>     write.csv(df, param_2) #> } #> <environment: 0x564e187a3fd0> # function(param_1 = "/data/input.csv", param_2 = "output.csv") { #   df <- read.csv(param_1) #   write.csv(df, param_2) # }  # --- Local bindings are protected --- x <- 42 expr2 <- expression({   x <- 1   y <- x + 1 }) fn2 <- from_example_to_function(expr2) # x is assigned inside the block, so it is NOT parameterized # even though x = 42 exists in the environment

what: Derive an iteration mask template from a function's signature — from_function_to_mask
title: Derive an iteration mask template from a function's signature — from_function_to_mask
text: Takes function (typically produced from_example_to_function()) returns one-row data.frame column corresponds parameter, default value cell value. "template mask" starting point user expands multi-row mask defines iteration cases.

title: Derive an iteration mask template from a function's signature — from_function_to_mask
code: from_function_to_mask(f)

title: Derive an iteration mask template from a function's signature — from_function_to_mask
text: f function whose formals define mask columns.

title: Derive an iteration mask template from a function's signature — from_function_to_mask
text: one-row data.frame one column per parameter. Parameters default values get values; parameters without defaults get NA.

title: Derive an iteration mask template from a function's signature — from_function_to_mask
text: genproc, mask data.frame row iteration case column parameter. function genproc() call user's function per row, passing column values arguments. from_function_to_mask() produces one-row template. user builds full mask adding rows (e.g. via rbind(), dplyr::bind_rows(), constructing multi-row data.frame directly).

title: Derive an iteration mask template from a function's signature — from_function_to_mask
text: scalar atomic defaults supported (character, numeric, integer, logical). Non-scalar defaults (vectors, lists, data.frames) supported future version via list-columns. extension preserve backwards compatibility: mask works today continue work unchanged.

title: Derive an iteration mask template from a function's signature — from_function_to_mask
text: mask returned pure data.frame parameter values. Metadata case_id, input file hashes, seeds managed separately genproc() execution time — stored columns attributes mask. design ensures standard data.frame operations (dplyr::filter(), [, rbind()) never accidentally strip metadata. mask later generalized dedicated class (genproc_mask), existing code passing plain data.frame continue work (backwards compatibility hard constraint).

title: Derive an iteration mask template from a function's signature — from_function_to_mask
code: fn <- function(input_path = "data.csv", n_rows = 100) {   head(read.csv(input_path), n_rows) } mask <- from_function_to_mask(fn) mask #>   input_path n_rows #> 1   data.csv    100 #   input_path n_rows # 1   data.csv    100  # Expand to multiple cases: full_mask <- data.frame(   input_path = c("a.csv", "b.csv", "c.csv"),   n_rows = c(10, 50, 100) )

what: genproc: robust, logged and reproducible iteration at organizational scale — genproc-package
title: genproc: robust, logged and reproducible iteration at organizational scale — genproc-package
text: genproc package wraps classical iteration constructs (, base::lapply(), purrr::pmap(), ...) orthogonal execution layers one-scripts can turned production-grade procedures without restructuring user's code.

title: genproc: robust, logged and reproducible iteration at organizational scale — genproc-package
text: Two layers always active (disabled): Logged: case produces structured log row real traceback (captured withCallingHandlers()) timing. Reproducibility: run records R version, loaded package versions, execution environment, exact mask used, hashes file inputs referenced mask, silent non-reproducibility runs can flagged user. layers planned (parallel, non-blocking, monitored, error replay). composable default layers opt-. package Shiny dependency intended reusable future companion Shiny package.

title: genproc: robust, logged and reproducible iteration at organizational scale — genproc-package
text: Maintainer: Daniel Rakotomalala rakdanielh@gmail.com

what: Run a function over a mask with mandatory logging and reproducibility — genproc
title: Run a function over a mask with mandatory logging and reproducibility — genproc
text: central function genproc package. takes function iteration mask (data.frame), calls function per row mask, returns structured result : log data.frame (one row per case, success/error/traceback/timing) reproducibility information (R version, packages, environment, parallel spec) exact mask used stable case IDs linking log rows mask rows

title: Run a function over a mask with mandatory logging and reproducibility — genproc
code: genproc(   f,   mask,   f_mapping = NULL,   parallel = NULL,   nonblocking = NULL,   track_inputs = TRUE,   input_cols = NULL,   skip_input_cols = NULL )

title: Run a function over a mask with mandatory logging and reproducibility — genproc
text: f function apply row mask. formal f correspond column mask (default value). Can produced from_example_to_function() written hand. mask data.frame row iteration case column parameter value. Can produced from_function_to_mask() expanded user. f_mapping Optional named character vector rename f's parameters execution. Passed rename_function_params(). Names current parameter names, values new names matching mask columns. parallel NULL (default, sequential execution) genproc_parallel_spec object produced parallel_spec(). supplied, cases dispatched workers via future.apply::future_lapply(). nonblocking NULL (default, synchronous call) genproc_nonblocking_spec object produced nonblocking_spec(). supplied, genproc() returns immediately genproc_result status "running", run continues background future. Use status() poll state await() block resolution. Can combined parallel — non-blocking wrapper envelops parallel dispatch. track_inputs Logical. TRUE (default), genproc detects columns mask reference input files records size + mtime result$reproducibility$inputs. Use diff_inputs() compare two runs detect silent input drift. Set FALSE skip input tracking entirely. input_cols NULL (default) character vector mask column names. supplied, heuristic detection bypassed exactly columns tracked. Paths exist capture time recorded NA size/mtime warning emitted. Mutually exclusive skip_input_cols. skip_input_cols NULL (default) character vector mask column names exclude heuristic detection. Useful label column happens match existing file. Mutually exclusive input_cols.

title: Run a function over a mask with mandatory logging and reproducibility — genproc
text: object class genproc_result (named list) components: log data.frame one row per case. Contains parameter values, plus case_id, success, error_message, traceback, duration_secs. reproducibility list environment information captured run start (R version, packages, OS, locale, timezone, mask snapshot, parallel spec ). See capture_reproducibility(). n_success Integer, number successful cases. n_error Integer, number failed cases. duration_total_secs Numeric, total wall-clock time entire run. status Character. "done" synchronous runs. Future execution layers (non-blocking) may return "running" "error" . genproc_result class designed forward compatibility. Existing fields (log, reproducibility, n_success, n_error, duration_total_secs) guaranteed stable. Future versions may add new fields (e.g. worker_id log parallel runs, collect()/poll() methods non-blocking execution) never remove rename existing ones.

title: Run a function over a mask with mandatory logging and reproducibility — genproc
text: logged reproducibility layers always active disabled. parallel layer optional: pass parallel_spec() parallel enable .

title: Run a function over a mask with mandatory logging and reproducibility — genproc
text: Cases executed sequentially row order default. Supply parallel = parallel_spec(...) dispatch parallel via future ecosystem. logging reproducibility layers remain active modes; parallel layer strictly additive. Parallel execution preserves mask row order resulting log data.frame, regardless order workers return. Parallel execution requires genproc installed (loaded via devtools::load_all()) worker, logging layer serializes closures whose environments reference genproc internals. exception parallel_spec(strategy = "sequential"), runs current process needs nothing extra — recommended mode deterministic testing.

title: Run a function over a mask with mandatory logging and reproducibility — genproc
text: Errors individual cases stop run. case wrapped add_trycatch_logrow(), captures error message real traceback (via withCallingHandlers). run continues next case. holds identically sequential parallel mode.

title: Run a function over a mask with mandatory logging and reproducibility — genproc
text: row mask receives case_id (currently index-based: case_0001, case_0002, ...). ID appears log can used replay, monitoring, cross-referencing.

title: Run a function over a mask with mandatory logging and reproducibility — genproc
text: mask need contain column every parameter f. Parameters present mask use default values. However, parameters without defaults also missing mask cause error execution starts. Extra columns mask (matching parameter) silently ignored.

title: Run a function over a mask with mandatory logging and reproducibility — genproc
code: # Sequential (default) result <- genproc(   f = function(x, y) x + y,   mask = data.frame(x = c(1, 2, 3), y = c(10, 20, 30)) ) result$log #>     case_id x  y success error_message traceback duration_secs #> 1 case_0001 1 10    TRUE          <NA>      <NA>             0 #> 2 case_0002 2 20    TRUE          <NA>      <NA>             0 #> 3 case_0003 3 30    TRUE          <NA>      <NA>             0  # Parallel — uses whatever future::plan() is currently set if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   future::plan(future::multisession, workers = 4)   result <- genproc(     f = slow_function,     mask = big_mask,     parallel = parallel_spec(seed = 42L)   ) } # }  # One-off parallel call, temporary plan, restored on exit if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   result <- genproc(     f = my_fn,     mask = my_mask,     parallel = parallel_spec(strategy = "multisession", workers = 4)   ) } # }  # Non-blocking: return immediately, keep the console, collect later if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   job <- genproc(     f = slow_fn,     mask = big_mask,     nonblocking = nonblocking_spec()   )   status(job)              # "running" or "done"   job <- await(job)        # blocks until resolution   job$log } # }  # Parallel + non-blocking composed if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   job <- genproc(     f = slow_fn,     mask = big_mask,     parallel    = parallel_spec(workers = 6),     nonblocking = nonblocking_spec()   )   # do other work here   job <- await(job) } # }

what: Specify a non-blocking execution strategy for genproc() — nonblocking_spec
title: Specify a non-blocking execution strategy for genproc() — nonblocking_spec
text: Returns configuration object pass nonblocking argument genproc(). supplied, genproc() returns immediately genproc_result status "running" actual work continues background future. Use status() poll state await() block completion.

title: Specify a non-blocking execution strategy for genproc() — nonblocking_spec
code: nonblocking_spec(strategy = "multisession", packages = NULL, globals = TRUE)

title: Specify a non-blocking execution strategy for genproc() — nonblocking_spec
text: strategy Character, NULL. One "sequential", "multisession", "multicore", "cluster". Default "multisession". Unlike parallel_spec(), default NULL: function named "non-blocking" must silently block current future::plan() sequential. Pass strategy = NULL explicitly defer caller's plan. "sequential" accepted mainly deterministic testing — exercises code path actually free console. packages Character vector, NULL. Extra packages attach background worker running. genproc attached automatically every strategy "sequential". globals Logical character. Forwarded future::future()'s globals argument. Default TRUE enables automatic detection.

title: Specify a non-blocking execution strategy for genproc() — nonblocking_spec
text: list class "genproc_nonblocking_spec" validated, normalized fields.

title: Specify a non-blocking execution strategy for genproc() — nonblocking_spec
text: nonblocking_spec() parallel_spec() orthogonal can combined. non-blocking layer launches one outer future; inside , parallel layer dispatches cases via future.apply. strategies set "multisession", future resolves inner layer "sequential" default (see future::plan() nesting rules) unless caller installs explicit nested plan via future::plan(list(...)).

title: Specify a non-blocking execution strategy for genproc() — nonblocking_spec
code: # Launch in the background, keep the console if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   spec <- nonblocking_spec()   job <- genproc(f = slow_fn, mask = mask, nonblocking = spec)   status(job)           # "running"   job <- await(job)     # blocks until done   job$log } # }  # Deterministic test: exercise the code path without real async spec <- nonblocking_spec(strategy = "sequential")

what: Specify a parallel execution strategy for genproc() — parallel_spec
title: Specify a parallel execution strategy for genproc() — parallel_spec
text: Returns configuration object pass parallel argument genproc(). object describes parallelize; actual execution carried future.apply::future_lapply() top backend selected future::plan().

title: Specify a parallel execution strategy for genproc() — parallel_spec
code: parallel_spec(   workers = NULL,   strategy = NULL,   chunk_size = NULL,   seed = TRUE,   packages = NULL,   globals = TRUE )

title: Specify a parallel execution strategy for genproc() — parallel_spec
text: workers Integer >= 1, NULL. Number workers use. Ignored strategy = "sequential". NULL, current future::plan() decides. strategy Character, NULL. One "sequential", "multisession", "multicore", "cluster". NULL (default), current future::plan() used unchanged. specified, genproc temporarily sets corresponding plan run restores previous plan exit. chunk_size Integer >= 1, NULL. Number iteration cases bundled per future. Larger values reduce scheduling overhead cost load-balance granularity. NULL delegates future.apply's default heuristic. seed Controls reproducible random-number generation across workers. Passed future.apply::future_lapply()'s future.seed argument. Default TRUE derives independent L'Ecuyer-CMRG streams random master seed. single integer fixes master seed. FALSE disables reproducible RNG recommended unless user function known RNG-free. packages Character vector, NULL. Extra packages attach worker running user function. genproc attached automatically every strategy "sequential". globals Logical character. Forwarded future.apply::future_lapply()'s future.globals. Default TRUE enables automatic detection, correct almost cases. Set character vector override detection.

title: Specify a parallel execution strategy for genproc() — parallel_spec
text: list class "genproc_parallel_spec" validated, normalized fields.

title: Specify a parallel execution strategy for genproc() — parallel_spec
text: "sequential": runs current process, workers. Exercises parallel code path without overhead; useful deterministic testing. "multisession": portable (works Windows), launches R subprocesses via parallelly. default recommendation workloads. "multicore": forks current process (Unix/macOS , available Windows reliable inside RStudio). Faster startup multisession loses portability. "cluster": explicit cluster workers, possibly machines. large-scale batch execution. users, leaving strategy = NULL calling future::plan() top session cleanest setup.

title: Specify a parallel execution strategy for genproc() — parallel_spec
text: seed = TRUE, case receives independent L'Ecuyer-CMRG stream derived random master seed. master seed -> identical results regardless worker count chunking. pin master seed, pass integer (seed = 42L).

title: Specify a parallel execution strategy for genproc() — parallel_spec
code: # Use whatever plan the caller has set spec <- parallel_spec()  # One-off parallel call with 4 workers, reproducible RNG spec <- parallel_spec(workers = 4, strategy = "multisession",                       seed = 42L)  # Exercise the parallel code path deterministically in a test spec <- parallel_spec(strategy = "sequential")

text: body contains nested function definition whose formals shadow parameter renamed, shadowed references inner body still renamed. unlikely practice (parameters from_example_to_function() named param_N) noted completeness.

title: Rename the parameters of a function — rename_function_params
code: fn <- function(param_1 = "in.csv", param_2 = "out.csv") {   df <- read.csv(param_1)   write.csv(df, param_2) }  fn2 <- rename_function_params(fn, c(   param_1 = "input_path",   param_2 = "output_path" )) # fn2 now has formals input_path and output_path, # and the body uses those names.

title: genproc 0.0.0.9000 (development)
text: Logged — structured log real traceback (captured via withCallingHandlers()) per-case timing. Reproducibility — environment snapshot run start (R version, platform, loaded package versions, mask, specs optional layer used). New parallel_spec() parallel argument genproc(): optional parallel dispatch future.apply::future_lapply(). Auto-defaults "multisession" workers passed without explicit strategy, restoring previous plan exit. New nonblocking_spec() nonblocking argument genproc(): genproc() returns immediately genproc_result status "running" run continues background future. Use status() poll, await() block resolution. Composable parallel. reproducibility layer now records stat-based fingerprint (size + mtime) every input file referenced mask. Stored result$reproducibility$inputs (method, files, refs). Heuristic detection default; explicit override via genproc(..., input_cols = ...) skip_input_cols = .... Disable track_inputs = FALSE. New diff_inputs(r0, r1) compares input fingerprints two runs reports changed / unchanged / added / removed files, human-readable print method.

title: genproc 0.0.0.9000 (development)
text: New S3 class genproc_result stable fields: log, reproducibility, n_success, n_error, duration_total_secs, status. Per-case errors stop run; captured log surfaced n_error. case_ids index-based (case_0001, …) now; content-based variant planned.

title: genproc 0.0.0.9000 (development)
text: from_example_to_function(): transform example expression parameterized function (modular AST rewrite engine, rlang dependency). from_function_to_mask(): derive one-row template mask (data.frame) function’s signature. rename_function_params(): rename parameters formals body. add_trycatch_logrow(): low-level logging wrapper used genproc().
